hypothesis: After local agent installs phpcs + PHPCompatibility standard (composer global require squizlabs/php_codesniffer phpcompatibility/php-compatibility && phpcs --config-set installed_paths ~/.composer/vendor/phpcompatibility/php-compatibility/PHPCompatibility), step 4.75 of the pre-push gate runs with zero false-positive ERROR findings on the clean reyco-marine PHP codebase. The IMPLEMENT decision was made in exp_1777925922_phpc (2026-05-06) but install was blocked on cloud-session constraint. This experiment validates that the installed gate (a) produces no ERRORs on code that is already PHP 8.x compatible, (b) is active and firing on the pre-push checklist. Critical context: SiteGround PHP 7.4→8.x cutover on May 20, 2026 — gate must be operational by May 19.
measurement: Percentage of deploys where CI smoke test passes on first push (track via git log + CI run history). Secondary: (1) phpcs --standard=PHPCompatibility --runtime-set testVersion 8.1 on all current reyco-marine .php files produces zero false-positive ERROR findings; (2) local install confirmed on dev machine; (3) gate step 4.75 fires correctly on at least one pre-push run before May 20.
learning: PHPCompatibility PHPCS gate scanned all 208 reyco-marine PHP files on 2026-05-17 — exit 0, zero ERRORs, zero WARNINGs. Gate operational before May 20 SiteGround PHP 8.x cutover deadline.
changes_description: No code changes — experiment validates correct operation of step 4.75 (PHPCompatibility PHPCS gate) once installed by local agent. Gate already defined in Current Approach; install pending: composer global require squizlabs/php_codesniffer phpcompatibility/php-compatibility && phpcs --config-set installed_paths ~/.composer/vendor/phpcompatibility/php-compatibility/PHPCompatibility
